novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The closest published prior art is missing: ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 on proximal inference without uniqueness and GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 on partial identification using proxy variables.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2
novelty_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.1's SC10 arithmetic for the scalar calibration value is wrong: with h0=h1=1/4, 1/5-(3/4)(1/4)-(1/4)(1/4)=-1/20, not 3/40.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The load-bearing witness value claimed in Theorem 2 and Exhibit 9.1 does not recompute; the scalar calibration value is not 3/40 under the displayed substitution.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The proof route is only definition unfold plus compact-line-segment affine-image algebra, so it has fewer than three substantive steps and cannot carry flagship weight.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Recompute the scalar NC-DiD calibration definition and either correct Theorem 2's displayed value or replace the witness with raw cells whose scalar calibration is actually 3/40.

Add ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 and GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 as primary comparators, then state exactly why the proposed interval is not covered by nonunique-bridge linear-functional identification or existing proxy partial-identification bounds.

To reach flagship rather than field tier, upgrade Conjecture 1 from a binary finite bounded-bridge LP image to a strict frontier theorem against the nonunique-bridge literature, with an observable necessary-and-sufficient condition for when the bridge functional is singleton versus interval-valued.

recommended_next_step: Reject this version: fix the failed witness arithmetic and pivot the literature gap around nonunique proximal bridges and proxy partial identification before resubmitting.
